summary.intent: Fix a bug (GH-458) where importing from OpenClaw overwrites the commented config template in moltis.toml, replacing ~700 lines of documented options with a bare ~20-line config.
summary.outcome: Successfully fixed by redirecting the local `save_config_to_path` in openclaw-import to delegate to `moltis_config::loader::save_config_to_path`, which uses `toml_edit` to merge values while preserving comments. Added a regression test and all 127 tests pass with no clippy warnings.

repo: moltis_config::loader::save_config_to_path (line 981) uses toml_edit to merge new values into existing TOML documents while preserving comments — this is the canonical comment-preserving save path.
openclaw-import has its own local save_config_to_path (lines 496-503) that did raw toml::to_string_pretty() + fs::write(), bypassing the comment-preserving path in moltis_config.
The toml crate dependency in openclaw-import must be retained because it is used in load_or_default_config and test code (toml::from_str), not just the now-replaced serialization path.
The openclaw-import Error type has a FromMessage variant that allows mapping foreign errors via .to_string().

code.finding: Local save_config_to_path that did raw toml::to_string_pretty() + fs::write() — replaced to delegate to moltis_config::loader::save_config_to_path.
moltis_config::loader::save_config_to_path is the comment-preserving save function using toml_edit; falls back to plain fs::write() when the file doesn't exist yet.
Error enum has a Toml variant derived from toml::ser::Error; considered removing it since direct serialization was eliminated, but retained to avoid breaking external matches.

workflow: Check all usages of a dependency before removing it — toml crate stayed because of test and non-serialization usages.
When delegating to a library function, verify its error type and available conversion paths before writing the wrapper.

friction: The Toml/ser::Error variant in the error enum created ambiguity about whether it could be safely removed after eliminating the direct toml::to_string_pretty() call — required checking all usages before deciding to keep it.
open_items: The toml::ser::Error variant in openclaw-import's Error enum may now be dead code (no production code produces it); could be removed in a follow-up cleanup if confirmed unused.
The 'no file yet' edge case (import runs before discover_and_load creates the template) is handled by moltis_config's fallback but has no dedicated test coverage.
